caption: The video appears to be a screen recording of an IRS webpage discussing Required Minimum Distributions (RMDs) for individuals over age 72. The page explains that RMDs must be taken by April 1 of the year following the year they reach age 72, or when they are no longer employed, depending on the plan's rules. It also mentions that 5% owners of the employer must start taking RMDs at age 72 and that RMDs cannot be rolled over to another IRA or retirement plan. The video highlights the "RMD Comparison Chart" and notes that Roth IRAs do not require distributions while the original owner is alive. Additionally, it covers RMD calculations and the 50% tax penalty on missed distributions, emphasizing the responsibility of IRA trustees or plan administrators to report or calculate the RMD amount, and the requirement for IRA owners to calculate RMDs separately for each IRA owned.
